steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage sets up a logical completion: most studies outside North America replicated the positive DOC-mercury association, and few matched Porvari and Verta's null result. The blank must follow from this evidence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'levels of dissolved organic carbon and mercury in bodies of fresh water are both much higher in Finland than elsewhere': The passage compares correlation patterns, not absolute concentration levels. Nothing in the text supports a claim about Finland having higher levels. ✗
  • Choice B — 'the hypothesis that the positive association is particular to North America is correct': This directly contradicts the evidence: multiple non-North-American studies (e.g., Luo in China) reproduced the positive association, undermining the North America-specific hypothesis. ✗
  • Choice C — 'dissolved organic carbon and mercury levels do typically rise and fall together in fresh water': This follows logically. Most studies across different regions found the same positive association; Porvari and Verta are the outlier. The accumulated evidence supports a general relationship. ✓
  • Choice D — 'there were circumstances unique to Luo and colleagues' study that impeded accurate measurements of mercury levels': The passage presents Luo's study as corroborating evidence, not as methodologically flawed. No basis for this claim is given. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
